ab: itiniay i Posong ko loma’ niyam i kakaemangan ho, mitilid to haw i, mafolaway to a tayra i Taypak, aitira sanay to i tokay ko ’orip.
ch: 小時候我們家住在台東，上學以後，就搬到了台北，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Posong. After I started school, we moved to Taipei and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: mitilid to i ta’angayay a pitilidan i, minanamay kako tono serangawan no Yincomin, mafana’ to kako to kahirahira, kadofah no serangawan no Taywan.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: saka, anini a mihecaan to pipa’aliwacan to kaciherangan, minokay kako ato kaka ako a tayra i niyaro’ a milicay to malitengay/mato’asay to sakafana’aw to serangawan no niyah a finacadan.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: faloay to a polo’ ko mihecaan nora mato’asay, nengneng han hato kaemangay ho.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: o manan manan a sowalan ko kafana’an iso a somowal han ako a milicay cingra, tanestesay ko sowal ningra tono ’Amis, mafana’ aca a somowal tono Piyoma, payrang ato no Dipong.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的阿美語說得很流利，也會說卑南語、閩南語和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in ’Amis, as well as Pinuyumayan, Hokkien and Japanese.

ab: licay han ako cingra a mafana’ kako, yo kaemang ho cingra i, minanamay to to sowal no Dipong, o Piyoma ko fafahi ningra, alomanay aca ko kananaman ningra a Payrang i katayalan saka, adihay ko kafana’an ningra a sowal.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是卑南族人，而他工作上認識許多閩南人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Pinuyumayan, and he knows many Hokkien at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: itini to kalalicay niyam, adihay ko makitohay no mato’asay to pakiniay to fenek no mi’adopay.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: talacowa cowa katadafana’ kami a mitengil nika, tanotingwaan no niyam a misasing nga’, nga’ay ko pisaliyaliyaw no niyam a mitengil.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: cowa kafana’ kako to serangawan no niyah i kakaemangan ho, pakini to no niyah o Yencumin a tireng i, mangodoay to ko harateng.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: ikor to haw i, mafana’ to kako to tireng no niyah o ’Amis, o tadamaanay o micidekay, o kalita’angan to konini!
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為阿美族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a ’Amis is unique and something to be proud of.
